summary: Gruber argues that Apple's entire company focus has shifted to competing with Android, with iPhone OS 4.0's major features being direct responses to Android's advantages. He explains that the new multitasking system is essentially a catch-up feature that closely mirrors Android's existing model, despite Apple pitching it as innovative. The iAds platform represents Apple's strategy to attack Google's core revenue stream — not through search, but through emotionally-driven mobile advertising that plays to Apple's design strengths. Gruber frames the entire event as a calculated series of moves against Google, from multitasking to advertising to privacy messaging.
key_points: Mac OS X 10.7 development was deprioritized as Apple redirected resources to compete with Android on mobile
iPhone OS 4's multitasking is functionally very similar to Android's existing model — pause/resume with background threads for specific tasks — despite Apple presenting it as original
iAds attacks Google not through search but through advertising, arguing mobile ads should be emotional and design-driven rather than text-based and algorithmic
The 128 MB RAM limitation in older devices is the primary reason the iPhone 3G won't get multitasking, not CPU speed
Nearly every tentpole feature announced was a direct competitive response to Android or a strategic attack on Google
money_quotes: The number one priority at Apple is to grow mobile market share faster than Android. Anything that is not directly competitive with Android is on the back burner.
If you want to fuck with Google, you go after advertising revenue.
It doesn't make much sense for Apple to take on Google in search, given Google's tremendous lead in the space and Apple's utter lack of expertise in the field.
Adding it now takes away the first item on the Android-vs.-iPhone talking points list. (And despite its similarities to Android's model, Apple is, of course, pitching it as original and innovative.)
Google ads may well be effective, but they are not emotional. Consider the Toy Story 3 iAd Jobs demoed. What kind of ad through Google could compare to that?
Some advertising, no matter the medium — TV, newspaper, magazine — is junk. But some is art.
If Microsoft still had the set of balls it had in the 90s, Internet Explorer would have been updated years ago to block web ads by default, including those from Google.
Jobs's message: You can trust Apple.
key_insight: iPhone OS 4.0 reveals that Apple has reorganized its entire company around a single strategic imperative: defeating Android by neutralizing its advantages and attacking Google's advertising revenue through design-driven mobile ads.
